message:
fix(runtime): blocking pool retirement CAS, io_driver ordering, reactor cleanup, timer wrapping

blocking_pool.rs: Replace unconditional fetch_add(1) for retirement
undo with a CAS loop that respects max_threads. Previously, a
retiring thread that found a queued task would blindly increment
active_threads, potentially exceeding the configured maximum. The
CAS loop now only un-retires if current < max_threads. Also removes
unreachable 'continue' after task completion signal.

io_driver.rs: Reorder PollingGuard::drop to restore events BEFORE
clearing is_polling. A concurrent thread could race in via the
is_polling CAS and observe an empty events buffer while ours was
still held in the guard.

epoll.rs + kqueue.rs: Always clean up bookkeeping (tokens/fds/regs
maps) regardless of whether the kernel deregister succeeded.
Previously, ENOENT/EBADF would clean up but other errors would
leak map entries, permanently blocking future registration of
reused FD numbers. Factored out the duplicated per-branch cleanup
into a single unconditional block after the match.

timer.rs: Fix generation comparison overflow by using wrapping_sub
+ cast_signed instead of direct cmp, so generation counters that
wrap around u64::MAX are compared correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
